raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 1.0,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which strengthening do you want prioritized: (A) rhetorical framing and language polish (R1.1, R1.6), (B) technical argument and claim–evidence alignment, possibly tempering/tightening claims (R1.2, R1.3), or (C) high-level structure and emphasis of the abstract (R1.5)? You can choose A, B, or C (or a combination).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Clarifying and sharpening the core thesis and contribution claims is the most direct way to make the piece feel stronger.",
    "R1.2": "Checking the coherence from assumptions (e.g., conditional independence) to Hoeffding-based bounds and the stated theorem strengthens the argument’s validity.",
    "R1.3": "Ensuring the empirical results and corollaries proportionally support the claims (e.g., 1/sqrt(K) rate and Brier improvements) prevents weak linkages.",
    "R1.5": "Adjusting section-level emphasis and ordering can foreground the main result and its implications for a stronger narrative arc.",
    "R1.6": "Tightening sentence-level clarity and concision bolsters perceived strength without changing content.",
    "RX.1": "We will surface assumptions and limits of generality to transparently qualify strengthened claims.",
    "RX.2": "We will avoid inflating claims beyond what the theorem and experiments warrant, especially around 'first' and generality statements.",
    "RX.3": "We will confine efforts to strengthening the argumentation and presentation of the provided text only.",
    "RX.4": "We will avoid wording that could enable misuse or overconfidence in uncertainty estimates in sensitive applications.",
    "RX.5": "We will maintain clear linkage between each claim and its source in the artifact (theorem, corollary, or empirical finding) for traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which strengthening do you want prioritized: (A) rhetorical framing and language polish (R1.1, R1.6), (B) technical argument and claim–evidence alignment, possibly tempering/tightening claims (R1.2, R1.3), or (C) high-level structure and emphasis of the abstract (R1.5)? You can choose A, B, or C (or a combination).

rationale.R1.1: Clarifying and sharpening the core thesis and contribution claims is the most direct way to make the piece feel stronger.
rationale.R1.2: Checking the coherence from assumptions (e.g., conditional independence) to Hoeffding-based bounds and the stated theorem strengthens the argument’s validity.
rationale.R1.3: Ensuring the empirical results and corollaries proportionally support the claims (e.g., 1/sqrt(K) rate and Brier improvements) prevents weak linkages.
rationale.R1.5: Adjusting section-level emphasis and ordering can foreground the main result and its implications for a stronger narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: Tightening sentence-level clarity and concision bolsters perceived strength without changing content.
rationale.RX.1: We will surface assumptions and limits of generality to transparently qualify strengthened claims.
rationale.RX.2: We will avoid inflating claims beyond what the theorem and experiments warrant, especially around 'first' and generality statements.
rationale.RX.3: We will confine efforts to strengthening the argumentation and presentation of the provided text only.
rationale.RX.4: We will avoid wording that could enable misuse or overconfidence in uncertainty estimates in sensitive applications.
rationale.RX.5: We will maintain clear linkage between each claim and its source in the artifact (theorem, corollary, or empirical finding) for traceability.
